message:
feat: tuning config constant consolidation (round 2), SSH config file override, and rate-limit window configurability

Tuning config constant consolidation (tuning_config.rs + 20 consumers):
- Continue the pattern from 03882e58 — extract named DEFAULT_ associated
  constants for every remaining TuningConfig struct: IngestTuning
  (max_persist_segment_bytes, max_record_payload_bytes), PolicyTuning
  (rate_limit_window_secs, max_tracked_panes, max_events_per_pane,
  cost_tracker_max_panes), AuditTuning (retention_days, approval_ttl_secs,
  max_raw_query_rows, artifact_retention_days, shadow_rollout_days),
  WebTuning, WorkflowsTuning, SnapshotTuning, and remaining subsystems.
- Wire all consuming modules to reference the canonical constants:
  agent_correlator, cass, cost_tracker, ingest, mcp, mcp_helpers,
  query_contract, rate_limit_tracker, recorder_audit,
  recorder_lexical_ingest, replay_artifact_registry,
  replay_shadow_rollout, rope, snapshot_engine, storage, tantivy_ingest,
  web, wezterm, workflows/descriptors, main.
- This eliminates the last category of magic-number duplication between
  Default impls and module-level constants.

Policy rate-limit window configurability (policy.rs):
- Replace hardcoded RATE_LIMIT_WINDOW Duration::from_secs(60) with
  DEFAULT_RATE_LIMIT_WINDOW sourced from PolicyTuning constant.
- Add `window: Duration` field to RateLimiter and RateLimitHit so the
  sliding window duration is observable in rate-limit diagnostics.
- Update RateLimitHit::reason() messages from "per minute" to
  "in {N}s window" to correctly reflect the configurable window.

Recorder audit expansion (recorder_audit.rs):
- Extend audit policy evaluation with additional checks.

SSH config file override (ssh.rs, mux/ssh.rs, sessioninner.rs, toml_config.rs):
- Add ssh_config_file: Option<String> to SshDomain for per-domain
  ssh_config path override. When set, ft resolves host defaults from
  this file instead of the standard search path and passes the override
  to the libssh session initialization.
- ssh_domain_to_ssh_config: use add_config_file() instead of
  add_default_config_files() when override is present. Also store the
  path as "frankenterm_ssh_config_file" in the ConfigMap so downstream
  session initialization can pick it up.
- SessionInner: apply the override during SSH session setup.
- toml_config.rs: wire the new field through TOML config parsing.
- Add test: ssh_domain_to_ssh_config_uses_override_config_file.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -193,6 +193,7 @@ fn build_policy_engine(config: &Config, require_prompt_active: bool) -> PolicyEn
         config.safety.rate_limit_global,
         require_prompt_active,
     )
+    .with_tuning(&config.tuning)
     .with_command_gate_config(config.safety.command_gate.clone())
     .with_trauma_guard_enabled(config.safety.trauma_guard.enabled)
     .with_policy_rules(config.safety.rules.clone())

@@ -99,6 +99,7 @@ pub(super) fn build_policy_engine(config: &Config, require_prompt_active: bool)
         config.safety.rate_limit_global,
         require_prompt_active,
     )
+    .with_tuning(&config.tuning)
     .with_command_gate_config(config.safety.command_gate.clone())
     .with_trauma_guard_enabled(config.safety.trauma_guard.enabled)
     .with_policy_rules(config.safety.rules.clone())
